item.title: Trump announces new effort to escort ships out of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump on Sunday announced Project Freedom, an effort to escort ships not involved in the war with Iran out of the Strait of Hormuz, will begin Monday.

item.title: A garden with 40 types of trees, shrubs and flowers... in a New York City lobby
item.description: Stopping to smell the roses does not just help you slow down and appreciate life, studies show it can also improve your mental and physical state. Michael George let's us in on a secret green space in Midtown Manhattan.

item.title: Unpacking the decline of Florida's citrus industry
item.description: The fruit that was once synonymous with the sunshine state is slowly disappearing. Cristian Benavides reports on the uphill battle against disease, development and natural disasters that Florida citrus farmers are facing.

item.title: 2 U.S. service members missing amid training exercise in Morocco
item.description: The service members were participating in African Lion, the largest joint military exercise on the continent.

item.title: Press gala shooting suspect moved off suicide watch, records show
item.description: A Monday hearing set to discuss removing Cole Allen from suicide watch has been cancelled, according to court documents.

item.title: Transcript: Sen. Raphael Warnock on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 3, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Sen. Raphael Warnock, Democrat of Georgia, that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 3, 2026.

item.title: Sen. Raphael Warnock: Supreme Court "poured fuel on this redistricting arms race"
item.description: Democratic Sen. Raphael Warnock of Georgia called the Supreme Court's decision last week to strike down Louisiana's congressional map and weaken the Voting Rights Act "a massive and devastating blow."

item.title: Watch a Spirit pilot's impromptu retirement celebration after airline shutters
item.description: A Spirit pilot received an impromptu retirement party from a different airline after what would have been his final flight was canceled.

item.title: 5/3: Sunday Morning
item.description: Hosted by Jane Pauley. Featured: The effects of overtourism; horse therapy; a tool to help keep dementia in check; Sting on "The Last Ship"; a golf journalist takes over a failing golf course; a Mozart exhibition; and collecting PEZ dispensers.

item.title: Ukraine hits Russian oil assets, including "shadow fleet" tankers
item.description: Ukraine has launched a wave of strikes against Russia's oil export infrastructure, including tankers in its "shadow fleet."

item.title: Chevron CEO says it's "very hard to predict" the Iran war's lasting impact on energy prices
item.description: In an interview on April 23, Chevron CEO Mike Wirth told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that it's "very hard to predict" the Iran war's lasting impact on energy prices "because markets are dynamic. They can respond to things that we don't anticipate."

item.title: Rep. Jason Crow says U.S. is "not good at having off ramps" in Middle East
item.description: Democratic Rep. Jason Crow of Colorado said on Sunday that the U.S should be considering a broader question of strategy in the war with Iran.

item.title: FDA expanding access to pancreatic cancer drug to some patients
item.description: The FDA this week announced it would be expanding access to a promising pancreatic cancer drug that nearly doubled survival time for patients with advanced pancreatic cancer in a large clinical trial.

item.title: Transcript: Rep. Jason Crow on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 3, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Rep. Jason Crow, Democrat of Colorado, that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 3, 2026.

item.title: Transcript: Minneapolis Fed president and CEO Neel Kashkari on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 3, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Minneapolis Fed president and CEO Neel Kashkari that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 3, 2026.

item.title: Face the Nation: Wirth, Crow, Warnock
item.description: Missed the second half of the show? Chevron CEO Mike Wirth, Rep. Jason Crow and Sen. Raphael Warnock join.

item.title: Rep. Jason Crow says U.S. is "not good at having off ramps" in Middle East conflicts
item.description: Democratic Rep. Jason Crow of Colorado, who is on the House Armed Services Committee, told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that the Iran war is the latest example of how the U.S. is "not good at having off ramps and accomplishing large strategic decisions in the Middle East."

item.title: Transcript: Kevin Hassett on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 3, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 3, 2026.

item.title: Minneapolis Fed president says U.S. needs to be "open-minded" about interest rates
item.description: Neel Kashkari, the president and CEO of the Minneapolis Fed, told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that while economists have been watching for an interest rate cut, "we all need to be open-minded about where interest rates are going because there's so much uncertainty coming out of the Middle East."

item.title: Sen. Warnock says voting rights decision "poured fuel on this redistricting arms race"
item.description: Sen. Raphael Warnock told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that the Supreme Court decision narrowing a section of the Voting Rights Act has "poured fuel on this redistricting arms race" as states have raced to undergo gerrymandering ahead of the 2026 midterm elections.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (May 3)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Video shows man's rescue after falling under bridge in Washington state
item.description: Dramatic video shows a man's rescue from beneath the High Steel Bridge in Washington state.

item.title: Kevin Hassett: Energy shocks will "affect profits for the airlines for the next quarter or so"
item.description: White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that although Spirit Airlines said rising fuel prices had contributed to its demise, "energy shocks don't have a big effect" on other airlines' business. But he acknowledged that "certainly, that will affect the profits for the airlines for a quarter or so."

item.title: 5/3: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," as gas prices skyrocket and Spirit Airlines shuts down, White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett and Minneapolis Fed president and CEO Neel Kashkari join. Plus, Sen. Raphael Warnock.

item.title: Sting embarks on "The Last Ship"
item.description: For centuries the English city of Newcastle was a hard-scrabble industrial powerhouse that built ships. It was also the hometown of the rock musician Sting, who as a young man witnessed the city's shipbuilding business dry up. He's paid homage to his town's heritage by writing and starring in a musical, "The Last Ship," which he's now taking on an international tour. He talks with Mark Phillips about his long career, and why he can't stop working.

item.title: Ford recalls more than 179,000 vehicles over front seat defect
item.description: Ford Motor Company is recalling over 179,000 vehicles due to a front seat issue that can increase the risk of injury in a crash, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said.

item.title: ICE reports 18th detainee death in 2026, putting agency on track for new record
item.description: ICE reported the 18th death of an individual in its custody so far this year, putting the agency on track to record a new all-time high in detainee deaths.

item.title: Ford CEO says company is focusing on making vehicles more affordable
item.description: Ford CEO Jim Farley tells CBS News, "Most of our new models are going to be more affordable versions."

item.title: Trump administration to cut 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany
item.description: President Trump said earlier this week he was reviewing the possible reduction of troops in Germany, which hosts more than 35,000 U.S. service members.

item.title: Former federal prosecutors see legal flaws in DOJ's SPLC indictment
item.description: Former federal prosecutors think the indictment struggles to articulate the elements of the alleged crimes in the case, a problem that could lead to its full or partial dismissal.
